title: Taming the Black Swan: A Momentum-Gated Hierarchical Optimisation Framework for Asymmetric Alpha Generation
abstract: This paper introduces AEGIS (Adaptive Equity Generation and Immunisation System), a momentum-gated portfolio framework applied to S&P 500, NASDAQ, and DJIA stock universes. A volatility-adjusted momentum filter screens for assets with smooth, monotonic price appreciation (analogous to a localised Sharpe ratio), while a minimax correlation algorithm enforces structural diversification. Convex optimisation (SLSQP) then allocates capital to maximise the Sortino ratio. A 20-year walk-forward backtest (2006–2025) across major stress events delivers CAGR of 15.41%, Sharpe of 0.72, max drawdown of −28.89%, and monthly win rate of 68.8% (18/20 profitable years), substantially outperforming the S&P 500 benchmark while matching NASDAQ-100 appreciation at lower downside volatility.
body:
Conventional momentum strategies, despite their proven efficacy in generating alpha, frequently suffer from the 'Winner's Curse', a structural vulnerability in which high performing assets exhibit clustered volatility and severe drawdowns during market reversals. To counteract this propensity for momentum crashes, this study presents the Adaptive Equity Generation and Immunisation System (AEGIS), a novel framework that fundamentally reengineers the trade-off between growth and stability. By leveraging a volatility-adjusted momentum filter to identify trend strength and employing a minimax correlation algorithm to enforce structural diversification, the model utilises sequential least squares programming (SLSQP) to optimise capital allocation for the Sortino ratio. This architecture allows the portfolio to dynamically adapt to distinct market regimes: explicitly lowering the intensity of crashes during bear markets by decoupling correlated risks, while retaining asymmetric upside participation during bull runs.

Empirical validation via a comprehensive 20-year walk-forward backtest (2006-2025), covering significant stress events including the 2008 GFC, confirms substantial excess alpha relative to the S&P 500 benchmark. The framework matched the capital appreciation of NASDAQ-100 while achieving significantly reduced downside volatility.

Key Results (TABLE III — 20-year walk-forward backtest 2006–2025):
- Total Gross Return: 1950.78%
- Total Net Return: 1657.17%
- CAGR: 15.41%
- Annualized Volatility: 16.44%
- Maximum Drawdown: -28.89%
- Sharpe Ratio: 0.72
- Average Sortino Ratio: 6.47
- Monthly Win Rate: 68.8%
- Profitable Years: 18/20
- Best Year (2020): +53.06%
- Worst Year (2008): -20.94%

Strategy Details:
- Data: S&P 500 Suite (large-cap), NASDAQ, Dow Jones components
- Signal: Volatility-Adjusted Momentum (VAM) filter — localised Sharpe ratio over look-back window L, prioritising smooth monotonic appreciation
- Diversification: Minimax correlation algorithm minimises maximum pairwise correlation in the selected basket
- Allocation: SLSQP convex optimisation maximising Sortino ratio
- Protocol: Walk-forward backtesting with strict in-sample/out-of-sample split; weights computed on [t-L, t], applied to [t, t+1]
- Benchmark: S&P 500 total return index
